2603.11938.title: Prototype-Based Knowledge Guidance for Fine-Grained Structured Radiology Reporting
2603.11938.paper_summary: Structured radiology reporting promises faster, more consistent communication than free text, but automation remains difficult as models must make many fine-grained, discrete decisions about rare findings and attributes from limited structured supervision. In contrast, free-text reports are produced at scale in routine care and implicitly encode fine-grained, image-linked information through detailed descriptions. To leverage this unstructured knowledge, we propose ProtoSR, an approach for injecting free-text information into structured report population. First, we introduce an automatic extraction pipeline that uses an instruction-tuned LLM to mine 80k+ MIMIC-CXR studies and build a multimodal knowledge base aligned with a structured reporting template, representing each answer option with a visual prototype. Using this knowledge base, ProtoSR is trained to retrieve prototypes relevant for the current image-question pair and augment the model predictions through a prototype-conditioned residual, providing a data-driven second opinion that selectively corrects predictions. On the Rad-ReStruct benchmark, ProtoSR achieves state-of-the-art results, with the largest improvements on detailed attribute questions, demonstrating the value of integrating free-text derived signal for fine-grained image understanding.
2603.11938.paper_summary_zh: 結構化的放射學報告承諾比自由文本更快且更一致的溝通，但由於模型必須對稀有發現和有限結構監督的屬性做出許多細微的、離散的決策，因此自動化仍然困難。相對而言，自由文本報告在常規護理中大規模生成，並通過詳細描述隱含地編碼細緻的、與影像相關的信息。為了利用這種非結構化知識，我們提出了ProtoSR，一種將自由文本信息注入結構化報告生成的方法。首先，我們介紹了一個自動提取管道，該管道使用經過指令調整的LLM來挖掘80k+ MIMIC-CXR研究，並建立一個與結構化報告模板對齊的多模態知識庫，通過視覺原型表示每個答案選項。利用這個知識庫，ProtoSR被訓練以檢索與當前影像-問題對相關的原型，並通過原型條件的殘差增強模型預測，提供一種數據驅動的第二意見，選擇性地修正預測。在Rad-ReStruct基準測試中，ProtoSR達到了最先進的結果，對於詳細屬性問題的改進最大，展示了整合自由文本衍生信號以實現細緻影像理解的價值。
2603.11938.author: Chantal Pellegrini et.al.
2603.11938.authors: Chantal Pellegrini, Adrian Delchev, Ege Özsoy, Nassir Navab, Matthias Keicher
